page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory capacity relative to compute resources. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 976 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.25 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations, fall within the average range. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI, which contribute to strong performance in database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m3-megamem-64 offers a high-density memory profile that reduces the need to scale out compute resources solely to acquire more RAM. This makes it a structurally efficient choice for memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, heavy data caching, and memory-intensive analytics workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 64 vCPUs and 976 GB of dedicated RAM, yielding 15.25 GB of memory per core. Powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 32 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs, prioritizing high-density memory configurations. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, excellent database operations, and high static web serving throughput, while single-core metrics and memory bandwidth remain average. The CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads, minimizing the need to over-provision compute resources. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and intensive data caching applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 976 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs and 976 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 976 GB of dedicated RAM providing 15.25 GB per core
Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 32 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Support for AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
